content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
October 30, 19 91
Ed Abercronbie
Planning Cornmissioner
298 East Bellevue Road
Aterater, cA 95301
Re:
Your Request for Advice
our FiIe No. A-91-4 71
Dear ur. Abercrombie:
This letter is in response to your request for confirmation
of the telephone advice provided to you on october 15, 1991,
regarding your duties and responsibi l ities under the conflict-of-
in€erest -piovisions of the Poiitical Reform Act (the 'Act").1
OUESTTON
As a plannj.ng connissioner for the city of Atnater, do the
conf lict-of-interest provisions of the Act require you to
disgualify yourself fron participating in governnental decisions
regarding a picnic shelter ln a local park?
CONCLUSION
The conf I ict-of-interest provisions of the Act do not require
you to disquatify yourself fron participating in decisions
iegarding the picnic shelter because you have no financial
interests in the proj ect.
FACTS
You were the president of the local Kiwanis club until
October, 1990. You received no cornpensation for your services to
the club. During that tine, the club was involved in sponsoring a
project to place a picnic shelter in a local park- You were
1t covernnent code sections 81ooo-91015. AII statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherrise indicated, cornmission
regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations - Section
18ooo, et seo. A1l references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 5 of the california code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO o P.O. Box 8O7 o Secramento CA 958044807 o (916) 322'5660


Our Plle No. A-91-{71
Page 2
lnstrumental in seeking approval of this project before the clty
councll and construction of the plcnlc shelter was connenced.
soon thereafter, a nenber of the pub1lc was successful in
preventLng the conpletlon of the project on the grounds that an
Envl.ronuental IDpact Report had not been conpleted. The project
is now scheduled for revl.ew by the planning conmission on october
30, 1991. You 6eek our advice to deternine whether the conflict-
of-interest provisions of the Act require you to disqualify
yourself fron participating in governnental decisions regarding
Lhis matter due to your prior involvement in this project.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits public officials fron making,
participating in, or using their of f ici.aI position to influence a
governmentat decision in which they know or have reason to know
they have a financial interest. As a rnember of the planning
cormnission of the City of At$rater, you are a public official.
(Section 82048. )
An official has a financial interest in a governrnental
decision within the rneaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision rri1l have a naterial financial
effect, distinguishable fron its effect on the public generally,
on the official, or on a mernber of the official's ironediate
fanlly, or on:
(a) Any business entity 1n which the public
official has a dlrect or lndirect investnent worth
one thousand dollars ($1rooo) or Dore.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars (91rO0O) or nore.
(c) Any source of incorne, other than gifts
and other than loans by a conmercial Iending
institution in the regular course of business on
tenos available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating tlro hundred fifty
dollars (5250) or roore in value provided to,
received by or pronised to the public official
within 12 nonths prior to the tine when the
decision is rnade '
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
enployee, or holds any position of nanagenent.
(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars (9250) or nore in value
provided to, recLived by, or pronised to the public


Our Fl■e No. A-9■-47■
Page 3
offlcial rrlthln 12 nonths prior to the tiroe vhen
the decislon Ls uade.
For purposes of this sectlon, lndirect
investnent or interest loeans any investment or
interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of
a public official, by an agent on behalf of a
publlc official, or by a business entity or trust
in which the official, the offlcial's agents,
spouse, and dependent children own directly,
indirectly, or beneficially a lo-percent interest
or greater.
Section 87103 .
Your facts indicate that you have received no incorne fron the
Xiwanis club for your participation in this project. Because you
have no econonic interests which night disgualify you from
participating in decisions regarding the picnic shelter, you nay
participate freely in all decisions regarding this project.
lve trust this tetter adeguately responds to your inguiry.
should you have any further questions reqalding this natter, do
not hesitate to caII ne at (916) 322-590]-,z
Sincerely,
scott Hallabrln
Acting ceneral counsel
counsel, Legal Division

coninission advice letters nay be oltained frorn the Connission at a
cost of 10C per Page.

conf lict-of-interest provisions of the Act require you to
disgualify yourself fron participating in governnental decisions
regarding a picnic shelter ln a local park?

CONCLUSION: The conf I ict-of-interest provisions of the Act do not require
you to disquatify yourself fron participating in decisions
iegarding the picnic shelter because you have no financial
interests in the proj ect.
